title: oxalis commutata
intro: Oxalis commutata, also known as the purple wood sorrel, is a perennial plant belonging to the wood sorrel family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its high concentration of oxalic acid, which acts as a natural diuretic. The plant has also been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and acting as an antioxidant, making it a potential treatment for conditions like arthritis and cardiovascular disease. Oxalis commutata can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using any herbal remedies.
